title: Citronella garden candle
subtitle: A soy wax outdoor candle with citronella essential oil in a wide jar
excerpt: Citronella essential oil at 8% of wax weight gives this outdoor candle a noticeable lemon-herbal fragrance and a mild deterrent effect against mosquitoes and midges. Soy wax container blend holds fragrance well and burns cleanly outdoors.
sourceNotes: Citronella candle formulation notes from Gracefruit fragrance-making guide (gracefruit.com, May 2026). Fragrance load percentages from soy wax manufacturer datasheet. Citronella insect deterrence from EPA registration data (epa.gov).

recipe.yieldDescription: 1 large garden candle (~400 g wax in a 600 ml jar)
recipe.batchNotes: Double or triple for a set of garden candles. Keep the fragrance load at 8% of wax weight regardless of batch size.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: Cure the candle for at least 3 days before the first burn for the best scent throw outdoors.
recipe.temperatureNote: Add citronella essential oil when the wax has cooled to 65 degrees Celsius. Essential oils added above 70 degrees Celsius lose volatile top notes before the wax sets.
recipe.shelfLifeNotes: 18 months. Citronella essential oil oxidises faster than most EOs, so the scent will fade before the wax burns out if the candle is stored for more than 18 months. Keep the lid on between uses and store away from heat and direct sunlight.

recipeTools.notes: Or two chopsticks held with a rubber band

glossaryTerms.definition: The percentage of fragrance or essential oil by weight added to a wax. Soy container wax holds 8 to 10% fragrance load. Above that, excess oil pools on the wax surface or causes poor burn performance.

The strength and radius of fragrance released by a burning candle. Hot throw refers to the scent when the candle is burning. Outdoor candles need a higher fragrance load than indoor candles because the scent disperses faster in open air.

content.text: Citronella candles work best outdoors where there is some air movement. The
disperses faster outside than indoors, which is why a higher

matters. Soy container wax comfortably holds 8% essential oil by weight, which is the right level for a noticeable outdoor deterrent effect. Citronella is toxic to cats, so keep these candles out of reach of pets and burn them in spaces cats do not frequent. A wide-mouth jar works better outdoors than a narrow vessel; the larger melt pool releases more fragrance and the low walls resist wind better.

Do not burn the candle on a wooden table, near fabrics or furniture, or anywhere a breeze could carry the flame sideways. Set it on a heat-resistant ceramic or stone surface and keep it clear of overhanging garden plants.

items.prepNote: 8% of wax weight; added at 65 degrees Celsius
CD-18 or wider for a large jar; size up for jars wider than 9 cm

content.text: Secure the pre-tabbed wick to the base of the jar with a small amount of melted wax or the metal tab adhesive. Centre the wick using a centring bar or two chopsticks held with a rubber band across the jar rim.

Melt the soy wax in the pouring pitcher set inside a saucepan of simmering water, or directly in a double boiler. Stir occasionally. Remove from heat when the wax is fully liquid, around 75 to 80 degrees Celsius. Allow to cool to 65 degrees Celsius, then stir in the citronella essential oil. Pour slowly into the prepared jar, leaving 1 cm of space at the top. Allow to set at room temperature undisturbed for a minimum of 8 hours. Trim the wick to 5 mm before the first burn.

Allow the first burn to melt the wax all the way to the edges of the jar before extinguishing; this takes around 3 to 4 hours for a wide jar. Setting this memory prevents tunnelling on subsequent burns.

Keep the lid on between uses to slow fragrance evaporation. Citronella essential oil oxidises faster than most other EOs; the candle performs best within 18 months of making. Store away from direct sunlight, which fades the essential oil faster. Never leave a burning candle unattended outdoors.
